title: Cold Jellyfish Salad

seo.title: Cold Jellyfish Salad Recipe | Authentic Chinese Appetizer
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make the best Cold Jellyfish Salad at home! This authentic Chinese appetizer is refreshing, savory, and perfect for any meal. Get the easy recipe!
longTailKeywords: how to make Cold Jellyfish Salad
Cold Jellyfish Salad step by step
best Cold Jellyfish Salad at home
h2Headings: How to Make Cold Jellyfish Salad: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Cold Jellyfish Salad
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of authentic Chinese Cold Jellyfish Salad, garnished with cucumber and cilantro, ready to serve as a refreshing appetizer.
seo.imageCaption: Prepare this refreshing Cold Jellyfish Salad, a classic Chinese appetizer that's perfect for a light and flavorful start to any meal.

description: Cold Jellyfish Salad is a quintessential Chinese appetizer, renowned for its unique crunchy texture and refreshing flavor. This savory dish features thinly sliced jellyfish tossed in a vibrant dressing of soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, and a hint of chili. It's the perfect light and invigorating start to any traditional Chinese meal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Jellyfish
instructions.text: If using dried jellyfish, rehydrate it according to package instructions, typically by soaking in cold water for several hours or overnight, changing water multiple times. Rinse thoroughly under cold running water, then blanch quickly in boiling water for 30 seconds if desired, immediately plunging into ice water to maintain crispness. Drain well and slice into thin strips if not already cut. Squeeze out excess water.

instructions.title: Prepare the Vegetables
instructions.text: Julienne the cucumber and carrot into thin, uniform strips. Mince the garlic and grate the ginger if using. Thinly slice the green onions and chop the fresh cilantro. Set aside in a large mixing bowl.

instructions.title: Whisk the Dressing
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the light soy sauce, Chinese black vinegar, sesame oil, granulated sugar, chili oil, and salt. Whisk everything together until the sugar and salt are fully dissolved and the dressing is well emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared jellyfish strips, julienned cucumber, carrot, minced garlic, grated ginger, sliced green onions, and chopped cilantro to the large mixing bowl containing the vegetables.

instructions.title: Dress and Toss
instructions.text: Pour the prepared dressing evenly over the jellyfish and vegetable mixture. Using tongs or clean hands, gently but thoroughly toss all the ingredients together, ensuring that every piece of jellyfish and vegetable is coated with the flavorful dressing.

instructions.title: Chill and Serve
instructions.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the Cold Jellyfish Salad for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and for the salad to chill properly. Before serving, give it another quick toss. Serve cold as a refreshing appetizer.

tips: For the best texture, ensure the jellyfish is thoroughly rehydrated and rinsed, then blanched very briefly (30 seconds maximum) before plunging into ice water. This preserves its signature crispness.

Experiment with different vinegars; while Chinese black vinegar offers authentic depth, a touch of rice vinegar can brighten the flavor. Adjust chili oil to your preferred spice level, or omit for a milder version.
This salad is best served well chilled, allowing the flavors to fully develop and making it even more refreshing. Prepare it at least 30 minutes in advance and keep it refrigerated until serving.
article: Cold Jellyfish Salad is a beloved Chinese Appetizers dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
